case_title: BOSCO CREDIT LLC VS. GEET PRASAD et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, SHORT CAUSE TRIAL SET FOR SEP-07-2010 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON MAY-07-2010 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BOSCO CREDIT LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT TO STIPULATION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND JUDGMENT THEREON IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $69,679.77 PLUS COSTS OF SUIT. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER AND SEPARATE [PROPOSED] JUDGMENT THAT TRACK THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLY WITH CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

[PROPOSED] DECLARATION OF DEFENDANT MAHENDRA PRASAD RE: OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND JUDGMENT THEREON (TRANSACTION ID # 62490167) FILED BY DEFENDANT PRASAD, GEET AKA GEET BALA PRASAD AKA GEET BALA AKA PRASAD GEET BALA PRASAD, MAHENDRA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GEET AND MAHENDRA PRASAD'S MOTION TO VACATE THE AUGUST 15, 2017 JUDGMENT IS GRANTED BASED ON CCP 473(D) AND EXTRINSIC MISTAKE. THE JUDGMENT IS SET ASIDE AND THIS CASE IS RESTORED TO THE CIVIL ACTIVE LIST. THE RECORD DISCLOSES THAT THE PRASADS HAD NO NOTICE THAT PLAINTIFF BOSCO CREDIT, LLC WAS ATTEMPTING TO ENTER JUDGMENT AGAINST THEM. IN ADDITION, THE JUDGMENT IMPROPERLY AWARDS PLAINTIFF NEARLY $70,000 WHEN THE STIPULATION ONLY ALLOWED FOR A JUDGMENT OF $20,000. THE STIPULATION DOES NOT PERMIT PLAINTIFF TO SEEK THE FULL AMOUNT OF THE ALLEGED DEBT IN THE EVENT OF A DEFAULT. EVEN IF THE STIPULATION PROVIDED FOR SUCH RELIEF, THE RECOVERY OF NEARLY $70,000 WOULD BE AN ILLEGAL PENALTY PER GREENTREE FINANCIAL GROUP, INC. V. EXECUTE SPORTS, INC. (2008) 163 CAL.APP.4TH 495. WHILE THIS MOTION COULD HAVE BEEN BROUGHT SOONER, COUNSEL FOR THE PRASADS PROVIDES AN ACCEPTABLE EXPLANATION FOR THE DELAY. COUNSEL FOR THE PRASADS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Monetary Judgment On The Grounds Said Judgment Is Void, Is Violative Of Due Process Principals, And Is The Result Of Extrinsic Fraud And Mistake
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GEET PRASAD, MAHENDRA PRASAD's Motion To Set Aside Monetary Judgment On The Grounds Said Judgment Is Void, Is Violative Of Due Process Principals, And Is The Result Of Extrinsic Fraud And Mistake.

Defendants Geet and Mahendra Prasad's motion to vacate the August 15, 2017 judgment is granted based on CCP 473(d) and extrinsic mistake. The judgment is set aside and this case is restored to the civil active list. The record discloses that the Prasads had no notice that plaintiff Bosco Credit, LLC was attempting to enter judgment against them. In addition, the judgment improperly awards plaintiff nearly $70,000 when the stipulation only allowed for a judgment of $20,000. The stipulation does not permit plaintiff to seek the full amount of the alleged debt in the event of a default. Even if the stipulation provided for such relief, the recovery of nearly $70,000 would be an illegal penalty per Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495. While this motion could have been brought sooner, counsel for the Prasads provides an acceptable explanation for the delay.

Counsel for the Prasads is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment To Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment And Judgment Thereon
Off Calendar Oct-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Angela Davtyan of Prober & Raphael (818-227-0100) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFF BOSCO CREDIT LLC'S Motion For Judgment To Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment And Judgment Thereon is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Plaintiff is entitled to judgment in its favor against all defendants in the amount of $69,679.77 plus costs of suit. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order and separate [proposed] judgment that track the Court's ruling, verbatim and that comply with CCP 437c(g).
